article_title: Study Finds 63 Percent of Veterinary AI Vendors Disclose No Validation Data as Industry Transparency Push Gains Momentum

content_preview.title: Study Finds 63 Percent of Veterinary AI Vendors Disclose No Validation Data as Industry Transparency Push Gains Momentum
content_preview.summary: A systematic audit of 71 commercial veterinary AI products published in Frontiers in Veterinary Science found a mean transparency score of just 6.4 percent, prompting at least one company to expand its public performance dashboard in response.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## A Market With Almost No Public Accountability

The commercial veterinary AI market in North America encompasses at least 71 products, yet the vast majority of vendors provide no public evidence that their tools work as advertised. A [systematic audit published in Frontiers in Veterinary Science on March 5, 2026](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/veterinary-science/articles/10.3389/fvets.2026.1761038/full) evaluated these products using a 25-point framework adapted from FDA and Good Machine...

editor_notes.content_quality: Good veterinary AI transparency article with systematic audit data.
editor_notes.source_verification: Frontiers in Vet Science, PR Newswire, JAVMA — all peer-reviewed/official.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Sourced from peer-reviewed audit of 71 commercial products.
